content: # Advanced AI System Test Document ## Overview This document contains information about advanced AI system features for comprehensive testing. ## Advanced Search Features ### Hierarchical Softmax Network (HSN) HSN is a neural network architecture that uses a hierarchical structure to efficiently compute softmax probabilities over large vocabularies. It reduces computational complexity from O(V) to O(log V) where V is the vocabulary size. Key benefits: - Faster training and inference - Reduced memory requirements - Scalable to large vocabularies - Better performance on rare words ### Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH) LSH is a probabilistic method for dimensionality reduction that preserves the locality of data points. It's particularly useful for approximate nearest neighbor search in high-dimensional spaces. Applications: - Document similarity search - Image retrieval - Recommendation systems - Anomaly detection ### Thomson Sampling Bandit Thomson sampling is a Bayesian approach to the multi-armed bandit problem. It balances exploration and exploitation by sampling from posterior distributions. Algorithm characteristics: - Optimal regret bounds - Easy to implement - Handles non-stationary environments - Works well with contextual bandits ### Adaptive Router The adaptive router dynamically selects the best model or service based on query characteristics and current system state. Features: - Real-time performance monitoring - Load balancing across services - Quality-aware routing - Cost optimization ## Performance Metrics - Response time: <100ms for simple queries - Throughput: 1000+ queries per second - Accuracy: 95%+ for domain-specific queries - Memory usage: <2GB for full model ## Test Cases 1. Simple keyword search 2. Complex boolean queries 3. Semantic similarity search 4. Multi-modal queries 5. Real-time streaming queries
